svg.text: Rings are buildable structures with docking ports for modules
Each ring is a physical truss you build, then fill with modules

g.text: Ring is a module type like any other.
Build it from the BUILD menu while docked.
Scaffolds like any module. Needs supply delivery.
Ring completion unlocks its module ports.
Ports render as empty docking clamps
until a module is built into them.
module_type_t type; // MODULE_RING or functional module
uint8_t slot; // port index within ring
Rings ARE modules. They live in the modules[] array.
A MODULE_RING in ring=0, slot=2 means "Ring 1 is built".
Ports only appear on completed (non-scaffold) rings.
Core (ring 0) always exists. Has dock + relay built-in.
Starter stations pre-populate rings + modules in world_reset().
